{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27542","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27542/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27542/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27542/events","html_url":"https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/27542","id":2581347874,"node_id":"I_kwDOAA3dP86Z3D4i","number":27542,"title":"ENH: Properly sanity check numpy.linalg.svd for memory issues","user":{"login":"croemheld","id":7149384,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjcxNDkzODQ=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/7149384?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/croemheld","html_url":"https://github.com/croemheld","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/croemheld/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/croemheld/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/croemheld/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/croemheld/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/croemheld/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/croemheld/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/croemheld/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/croemheld/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/croemheld/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-10-11T13:09:15Z","updated_at":"2024-10-11T13:09:15Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Proposed new feature or change:

I've come here after creating an issue in https://github.com/ajhynes7/scikit-spatial/issues/359.

It seems to me that when a matrix is too big for `numpy.linalg.svd`, then I either get a `init_gessd failed init` or a more precise message like `numpy.core._exceptions._ArrayMemoryError: Unable to allocate 79.5 TiB for an array with shape (3305428, 3305428) and data type float64`. Obviously, the latter one is easy to identify if the required space goes towards the absurd, but sometimes (like in the first case), the message seems to be also related to memory and size issues for `numpy.linalg.svd`, yet the function "fails" silently which I think is a bigger issue as no exception is happening.

The original issue uses numpy 1.26.4, but I've seen the same in version 2.1.2.

Would it be possible to add a (more precise) sanity check in `numpy.linalg.svd` that would maybe improve the message `init_gessd failed init` (for which nearly no useful posts exist on the internet), that explains why exactly it failed?

Thanks!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27542/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27542/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}
